openFindings.title: Lock refresh can overwrite a newly reclaimed holder
openFindings.description: A delayed heartbeat from a stale-but-still-running owner can resurrect that owner over a newer claimant, allowing two loop hosts to believe they own the same packet lock.

openFindings.title: Malformed lock files are never reclaimed
openFindings.description: An empty or malformed .deep-loop.lock can permanently block subsequent loop acquisition.

openFindings.title: Locked JSONL merge can overwrite unlocked appends
openFindings.description: A state-log event can be silently lost when a locked merge races an unlocked append to the same JSONL file.

openFindings.title: Review FILE coverage gaps use the wrong edge direction
openFindings.description: A normal review graph with a seeded SLICE/DIMENSION -> FILE COVERS edge can still report that FILE as uncovered because findCoverageGaps checks e.source_id = n.id for FILE nodes.

openFindings.title: Research claim verification query disagrees with convergence scoring
openFindings.description: The query and signal paths use different verified-claim predicates, so the same CLAIM row can be both verified for claimVerificationRate and unverified for findUnverifiedClaims.

openFindings.title: Round-state append corrupts a valid final JSONL line without a newline
openFindings.description: A state file ending with a valid JSON record but no trailing newline is considered unrepaired, and the next append corrupts the JSONL stream.

openFindings.title: Read-side JSONL repair ignores the writer lock
openFindings.description: The read path can mutate round-state JSONL without participating in the single-writer lock protocol, creating a data-loss race with active appends.

openFindings.title: Same-kind fanout replicas still share real CLI homes and lockfiles
openFindings.description: Same-kind cli-codex, cli-opencode, or cli-claude-code fanout replicas are not effectively state-isolated because the script sets only SPECKIT hints while preserving the real CLI home env vars the children use.

openFindings.title: baseArtifactDir can relocate fanout artifacts outside the canonical review tree
openFindings.description: A baseArtifactDir such as /tmp/outside-root passes validation and becomes the root for fanout state plus child-agent output instructions, so the script lacks a containment guard for artifact root resolution.

openFindings.title: cli-claude-code configDir accepts arbitrary traversal/absolute paths into CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
openFindings.description: A cli-claude-code lineage can supply a traversal or absolute configDir that is exported directly as CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR for the child process without containment checks.

openFindings.title: Continuation reservation failure leaves the session locked in memory
openFindings.description: A transient goal-state write failure during active-continuation reservation can permanently suppress continuation for that session until the plugin process restarts.

openFindings.title: Immediate judge rejection leaves fast-timeout timer alive
openFindings.description: Immediate model errors leak the fast timeout timer until it fires.

openFindings.title: Same-objective set can reactivate exhausted goal state
openFindings.description: Setting the same objective after a terminal or budget-limited state violates the set/replace contract and can inject stale active goal state.

openFindings.title: Injection clamping can remove the directive and closing marker
openFindings.description: Accepted objective/reason lengths can produce a malformed active-goal injection that omits both the directive and closing marker.

openFindings.title: Model-benchmark runs write reports without the reducer ledger row
openFindings.description: Normal non-reviewer /deep:model-benchmark runs can silently omit the benchmark_run ledger record, causing reduce-state to undercount benchmark runs and stop-status inputs.

openFindings.title: Workflow promotion still defaults to immediate canonical mutation
openFindings.description: The accepted-vs-shipped safety split is bypassed in the normal workflow commands because they invoke the promoter without --phase.

openFindings.title: Pre-mutation mirror sync blocks legitimate agent-definition promotion
openFindings.description: Agent-definition promotion can fail before mutation even when the candidate is valid, because the all-mirrors-in-sync check is performed against the future candidate before any mirror has been updated.

openFindings.title: Rollback ignores the stored accepted-state hash before restoring backup
openFindings.description: Rollback can silently corrupt the canonical target if the accepted-state backup file is modified, because the stored hash is available but unused.

openFindings.title: Corrupt existing lock files are neither reclaimable nor reported accurately
openFindings.description: A crash or partial write that leaves an empty/malformed lock file can permanently block acquisition instead of being reclaimed or surfaced as a corrupt lock.

openFindings.title: Deferred writer timer flush can create an unhandled rejection
openFindings.description: The normal debounce-driven flush path drops write failures on the floor as unhandled promise rejections, which can crash the process or hide a failed state write.

openFindings.title: Fan-out review subprocesses get repo-wide write power while isolation is only prompt-enforced
openFindings.description: Fan-out CLI lineages can write outside their intended artifact directory by default.

openFindings.title: Coverage graph metadata is accepted and re-emitted without redaction
openFindings.description: Coverage graph query output can leak secrets from arbitrary metadata.

openFindings.title: Review iteration template omits a field the validator requires
openFindings.description: The deep-review leaf iteration output contract and post-dispatch validator disagree on whether findingDetails is required.

openFindings.title: Diff-gated JSONL append can lose concurrent rows
openFindings.description: The append helper is atomic as a replace, not atomic as an append; concurrent writers can silently lose a valid JSONL row.

openFindings.title: Exit-zero salvage misses are reported as fulfilled lineages
openFindings.description: A lineage with unrecoverable missing iteration artifacts can be counted as successful when the subprocess exits 0, silently losing required review output.

openFindings.title: Reducer can drop findings when the delta file lacks per-finding rows
openFindings.description: The validator/reducer contract allows structured finding data to pass validation but be ignored by the registry builder, which can produce a false PASS or undercount active findings.

openFindings.title: setGoal can revive stale terminal state and carry budgets into replacement goals
openFindings.description: A normal /goal set can produce an active goal that still carries terminal-state accounting or an unintended previous budget, causing immediate budget stops or stale verifier/completion state.

openFindings.title: Usage dedupe only remembers the immediately previous message
openFindings.description: Repeated message.updated events are not reliably deduped unless duplicates are contiguous, so token accounting can be silently inflated in normal streaming or delayed-update scenarios.

openFindings.title: stateDir override can escape the goal state root
openFindings.description: The plugin prevents session-id path traversal via hex filename keying, but still allows state-dir traversal because the parent state directory override is resolved without an allowlist or base-directory check.

openFindings.title: Fan-out timeout and stop paths do not reliably terminate lineage subprocesses
openFindings.description: Fan-out lineages are not reliably terminated on timeout or parent signal, so normal timeout/interrupt handling can hang the runner or leave orphan writers racing later merge/resume steps.

openFindings.title: Post-dispatch validation can fail a valid new iteration when an older JSONL line is malformed
openFindings.description: A non-tail malformed JSONL line can make post-dispatch validation reject an otherwise valid appended iteration record.

openFindings.title: writeStateAtomic can write invalid JSON for non-representable top-level state
openFindings.description: A bad or missing state snapshot can be persisted as invalid JSON instead of failing closed before touching the target file.

openFindings.title: Deferred writer timer path can create unhandled write rejections
openFindings.description: Background persistence failures in the debounced writer escape the runtime's error taxonomy and can crash or disappear instead of being reported through flushNow()/close().

openFindings.title: Corrupt lock files become unreclaimable and misreport the contender as holder
openFindings.description: A crash that leaves an empty or malformed lock file can permanently block subsequent deep-loop acquisition and produce misleading holder metadata.

openFindings.title: Rejected prompt events clear the autonomous continuation blocker
openFindings.description: Rejected prompt actions can clear the only prompt-block guard and allow autonomous continuation after user rejection.

openFindings.title: Review prompt can produce JSONL that the validator rejects
openFindings.description: A prompt-compliant deep-review iteration can be rejected by the runtime validator because the producer contract and validator contract disagree on findingDetails.

openFindings.title: Fuzzy substring scoring lets very short aliases match at the default merge threshold
openFindings.description: Short substring aliases can produce default-threshold consolidation candidates even when the token-level fuzzy guard would have rejected them.

openFindings.title: Unsupported-verb and bare-text routing contracts conflict
openFindings.description: The command router gives incompatible instructions for unknown first tokens.

openFindings.title: Hub registry packet-name check reads the wrong schema location
openFindings.description: The D5 hub registry packet-name mismatch guard is ineffective for the live registry schema.

openFindings.title: Diff-gated JSONL append has no multi-writer coverage despite read-rewrite semantics
openFindings.description: The tests do not prove whether appendJsonlIfChangedAtomic is single-writer only or safe under concurrent status-ledger writers.

openFindings.title: The append-boundary concurrent test is sequential
openFindings.description: The jsonl-repair Vitest suite claims concurrent append-boundary coverage without actually running concurrent append writers.

openFindings.title: Default-export-only regression guard is not automated
openFindings.description: The plugin suite does not fully cover the default-export-only contract because it lacks a module-shape assertion that rejects named exports.

openFindings.title: Command router behavior is only manually covered
openFindings.description: The plugin suite does not cover the /goal command-router contract even though the command is part of the integrated goal surface.

openFindings.title: Tool-level fail-closed missing-session paths are not pinned
openFindings.description: The plugin suite does not prove the public tool handlers fail closed when ToolContext lacks a session id.

openFindings.title: Injection tests cover presence, not adversarial sanitization
openFindings.description: The plugin suite does not adequately cover injection hardening because it lacks adversarial objective/evidence cases.

openFindings.title: Continuation gate tests cover the main ladder but not several fail-closed gates
openFindings.description: The continuation tests do not adequately cover the full fail-closed gate ladder that controls autonomous prompt submission.

openFindings.title: Fan-out persists raw subprocess stdout for salvage without a redaction pass
openFindings.description: Fan-out salvage/debug artifacts can persist raw child stdout containing secrets.

openFindings.title: appendJsonlIfChangedAtomic is single-writer despite append-shaped API
openFindings.description: The helper's API/documentation overstates append safety and does not encode its single-writer precondition.

openFindings.title: JSONL merge writer omits fsync before rename
openFindings.description: The JSONL merge path is atomic against torn readers, but it does not provide the crash durability implied by the runtime's temp+fsync+rename state-safety contract.

openFindings.title: Pool abort-requeue lag action is not reachable from fanout-run
openFindings.description: The pool API and runner/config contract have drifted: abort-requeue behavior is tested in isolation but dead through the real fanout-run entrypoint.

openFindings.title: Unsupported-verb contract conflicts with bare-text routing
openFindings.description: The command markdown has a router contract drift that can surprise users and tests because unsupported verbs and bare objectives are not distinguishable under the current rules.

openFindings.title: Pool abort-requeue lag action is not reachable from fanout-run
openFindings.description: The pool API and runner/config contract have drifted: abort-requeue behavior is tested in isolation but dead through the real fanout-run entrypoint.

openFindings.title: Salvage writes the same recovered stdout to every missing iteration file
openFindings.description: The salvage path can create misleading per-iteration artifacts when more than one iteration file is missing, because it does not attribute recovered stdout to individual iterations.

openFindings.title: appendJsonlIfChangedAtomic can lose rows under concurrent writers
openFindings.description: The helper's atomic replacement protects against torn writes but not lost updates, so its name and append contract are unsafe for multi-process ledgers.

openFindings.title: Invalid mk_goal actions silently fall back to show
openFindings.description: A rejected or misspelled action can produce STATUS=OK ACTION=show instead of a failure envelope when executeGoalAction is reached without schema enforcement.

openFindings.title: Continuation budget-exhaustion gate lacks direct regression coverage
openFindings.description: A safety-critical budget-exhaustion branch can regress without a test that proves promptAsync is not called and the goal is marked budget_limited.

openFindings.title: Judge hardening leaves the fast-timeout timer active on immediate rejection
openFindings.description: Immediate judge rejection leaves the fast timeout active until it fires, creating a bounded event-loop leak/delay in the judge failure path.
